LAW AND MOTION 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT MR. BLAKE WHO REPRESENTED HIM IN DEFENDING A CHALLENGE FOR CAUSE FILED BY A PARTY IN THIS COURT A COUPLE OF YEARS AGO AND IS NOT REPRESENTING HIM AT THIS TIME. HE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD COMPLIANCE WITH THE TORT CLAIMS ACT AND TO PLEAD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WHICH THE DESIGNATED DEFENDANT'S ARE NOT IMMUNE, STATUTORY DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED WRONGFUL ACTS CLEARLY AND CONCISELY IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH C R C 2.112. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO CAUTIONED TO COMPLY WITH THE STATE AND LOCAL STATUTES AND RULES OF COURT CONCERNING THE FORMATTING, LENGTH AND TIMELINESS OF FILINGS, INCLUDING C C P SECTION 1005, C R C 2.108, 2.111 AND 3.1113 AND LOCAL RULE 2.6(B). PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 301/PJB

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF HASSAN GOLCHINI'S MOTION TO SETTLED STATEMENT IN LIEU OF THE REPORTERS TRANSCRIPTS IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING THE MOTION WAS OTHERWISE PROPER, ANY APPELLATE REVIEW OF THE COURT'S RULING ON THE PLEADING MOTIONS AT ISSUE WOULD BE DE NOVO BECAUSE ONLY QUESTIONS OF LAW BASED ON THE PLEADINGS THEMSELVES ARE PRESENTED. NO SETTLED STATEMENT IS NECESSARY OR APPROPRIATE. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189

Notice Of Motion To Settled Statement In Lieu Of The Reporters Transcripts
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HASSAN GOLCHINI'S MOTION TO SETTLED STATEMENT IN LIEU OF THE REPORTERS TRANSCRIPTS. Appearances: Tom Blake of the Office of the Attorney General (703.5506) for defendant and responding party; Hassan Golchini (673.7721) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Even assuming the motion was otherwise proper, any appellate review of the court's ruling on the pleading motions at issue would be de novo because only questions of law based on the pleadings themselves are presented. No settled statement is necessary or appropriate. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189
